definitions.technical: A bank or savings association whose deposits are insured by the FDIC. The GENIUS Act allows subsidiaries of insured depository institutions to become permitted stablecoin issuers.
definitions.lay: A bank whose customer deposits are protected by FDIC insurance. Under the GENIUS Act, companies owned by these banks can apply to become approved stablecoin issuers.
definitions.parenthetical: (an FDIC-insured bank or savings association whose subsidiaries may become permitted stablecoin issuers)
definitions.legal_significance: The GENIUS Act uses the insured depository institution framework as a gateway for stablecoin issuance, leveraging existing banking supervision infrastructure. Subsidiaries of these institutions benefit from established regulatory relationships and examination procedures.
